skills.file.write: Write text content to a file (creating parent dirs as needed).
skills.file.move: Move or rename a file. Source must exist, parent of destination must exist.
skills.file.copy: Copy a file. Source must exist, parent of destination must exist.
skills.file.delete: Delete a file. Idempotent — silent on already-missing.
skills.shell.rm: Remove a file or directory (rm -rf for dirs).
skills.shell.find: Find files by name pattern under a directory.
skills.shell.grep: Search file contents for a regex.
skills.shell.sed: In-place text substitution in a file.
skills.shell.head: Print the first N lines of a file.
skills.shell.tail: Print the last N lines of a file.

note: Phase 1 restricts to file-management skills only. Network, package management, and code execution are deliberately out-of-scope — they require either real workspace bytes (e.g., a real Python file that imports something), a working network egress in the sandbox, or richer verification than the five assertion primitives can express.
